title: A doctor once told her not to run. Now she’s taking on the Boston Marathon.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sydney Southern, 33, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon after recovering from a surgery at Mass General Hospital.
She was advised against running after surgery in 2014 but has been cleared for all activities following recovery.
Southern will run to support Mass General Hospital, which played a crucial role in her recovery and rehabilitation.

title: Cambridge pediatric doctor running fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Jennifer Leah Goetz, 39, from Cambridge, is running her fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients.
Goetz trained in pediatrics and child psychiatry at Massachusetts General Hospital, working with children battling cancer.
She began running to cope with depression and now runs to raise funds for Massachusetts General Hospital's programs.

title: Brothers unite for Boston Marathon run honoring family, cancer losses
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin and Conor Nally from Quincy will run the 130th Boston Marathon to honor family members lost to cancer.
Collin's father, Michael Nally, and fiancé Ryann’s mother, Jill Bailey, both passed away from cancer, inspiring his marathon journey.
The Nally brothers aim to use their marathon run to highlight the impact of cancer and cherish their physical abilities.

title: Mass. has one of the best minor league ballparks in America, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Polar Park in Worcester ranked No. 6 among the best minor league ballparks in the U.S. by USA Today.
Polar Park opened in 2021 and hosts the Worcester Red Sox, a team with strong ticket sales history.
Smiley Ball, the Worcester Red Sox mascot, was ranked No. 3 among the best minor league mascots.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (March 26, 2026)
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 7th Street Burger opens on Newbury Street, with another location coming to South Boston in April.
Far Out Ice Cream plans to open its fourth Greater Boston location in Cleveland Circle by April 19.
Gemma Kitchen & Bar set to debut in Norwood inside an old firehouse with an all-American, Italian-inspired menu.

title: State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley indicted in deadly Woburn crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley was indicted for motor vehicle homicide, accused of driving drunk in a 2023 fatal crash.
The collision resulted in the death of Angelo Schettino, a 37-year-old with developmental disabilities, and injured another individual.
Quigley's indictment follows renewed attention to the case, and he will be arraigned in Middlesex Superior Court at a later date.

title: Framingham’s iconic ‘castle’ hotel has entered a new era. Take a look inside.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The former Sheraton Framingham Hotel has reopened as The Framingham Hotel Collection after a multi-year renovation.
The Framingham Hotel Collection includes 371 rooms across Renaissance, Fairfield Inn & Suites, and TownPlace Suites brands.
Three new dining options include Malone's Irish Pub & Social and Provisions Hearth & Kitchen at the hotel campus.

title: With travel concerns rising, are you rethinking flights?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Concerns are rising at Logan Airport due to TSA staffing issues amid a partial Department of Homeland Security shutdown.
Union leaders warn staffing strains at Logan could lead to delays, highlighting impacts on worker morale and anxiety.
A recent crash at LaGuardia Airport involving overlapping failures raises additional safety concerns for air travelers.

title: This Boston hotel is giving music lovers a reason to travel
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Omni Boston Hotel at the Seaport offers an All Access Pass package including concert tickets, even for sold-out events.
Logan Airport introduces a new biometric system for TSA PreCheck flyers, allowing identity verification via facial recognition.
Coastal Maine Botanical Garden in Boothbay, the largest in New England, opens May 1 with giant art installations.

title: Tom Brady reveals that he has ‘explored a lot of different things’ about making an NFL comeback
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady, 48, has been retired for three years but explored a potential NFL return after acquiring a minority stake in the Raiders.
An NFL spokesperson stated Brady must sell his ownership in the Raiders to play, due to league policies against player ownership.
Brady remains active with other pursuits like broadcasting NFL games and participating in events like the Fanatics Flag Football Classic.
